zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine於1393年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾厄斯金時，她的父親William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine25歲，母親Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie17歲。她於大約1408年與Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan在蘇格蘭薩瑟蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1479年之後在蘇格蘭金洛-爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine于1393年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾厄斯金时，她的父亲William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine25岁，母亲Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie17岁。她于大约1408年与Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan在苏格兰薩瑟蘭结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1479年之后在苏格兰金洛-爾。

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine was born in 1393, in Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine, was 25 and her mother, Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, was 17. She married Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan about 1408, in Sutherland, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died after 1479, in Kinross-shire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine est née en 1393, à Erskine, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan environ 1408, à Sutherland, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1479, à Kinross-shire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine 1393 in Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine 25 und ihre Mutter Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie 17. Sie hat um 1408 in Sutherland, Scotland Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1479 in Kinross-shire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine è nata il nel 1393 nel Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland, sua padre, William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan circa 1408, nel Sutherland, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1479, nel Kinross-shire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Graham of Kincardineが1393年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエルスキンで生まれた時，父William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardineは25歳，母Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellieは17歳であった。約1408年にスコットランドススエルランドでSir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1479年以後にスコットランドキンロス-シュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine(이)가 1393년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 어스킨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1408년 스컷랜드 서덜랜드에서 Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1479년 이후 스컷랜드 킨로스-실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine nasceu em 1393, em Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan aproximadamente 1408, em Sutherland, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1479, em Kinross-shire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine родилась в 1393 г. в местности эрскаин, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan около 1408 г. в местности сутирланд, Шотландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1479 г. в местности кинросс-шир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Graham of Kincardine nació en 1393, en Erskine, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir John Stewart 1st of Auchingoun, Blackhall and Ardgowan aproximadamente 1408, en Sutherland, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1479, en Kinross-shire, Scotland.
